_note: Refreshed by the refresh-sports-feed scheduled task. Each item is {headline, url, source, published (ISO8601), snark}. Snark voice: Barstool-adjacent guy-at-the-bar, crass, horny, cocky, zero filler. One sentence. No slurs, no punching down at specific real people in a harmful way. Punch sideways (at media, front offices, rival fanbases) and up (at ownership). Keep it Boston.

items.headline: Red Sox at Blue Jays: Payton Tolle vs. Trey Yesavage tonight
items.snark: Tolle threw 11 Ks his last time out, Yesavage has a 7.50 rehab ERA, this should be a bloodbath.

items.headline: What's driving Red Sox's stunning turnaround? Three straight wins
items.snark: Fire your manager, win three straight. Correlation, causation, who cares, we're running with it.

items.headline: Red Sox add three interim coaches to staff after Cora massacre
items.snark: Chad Tracy's patching together a coaching staff like it's a beer-league softball roster.

items.headline: Red Sox at Blue Jays lineups: Fleeing the country
items.snark: Roman Anthony's back in the lineup. Not sure if that's a medical update or a hostage situation.

items.headline: Breslow open to feedback on communication style after Cora firing fallout
items.snark: Craig Breslow says he's open to feedback. Players say they're paid to shut up and play ball. Cool.

items.headline: Red Sox shutout Blue Jays 5-0 behind Ranger Suarez gem
items.snark: Suarez struck out 10 in eight innings of one-hit ball. Firing Cora unlocked something, apparently.

items.headline: Trevor Story takes stance on Chad Tracy as Cora's replacement
items.snark: Story says it's unfair to Cora but Tracy's a special baseball mind. That's called hedging, folks.

items.headline: Garrett Crochet believes Red Sox close to putting it together
items.snark: Crochet thinks we're close, like how I'm close to winning the lottery every week.

items.headline: Red Sox face Dylan Cease and Blue Jays tonight after stunning Cora firing
items.snark: Cease leads MLB in strikeouts, Sox just torched their coaching staff, this should go well.

items.headline: Red Sox at Blue Jays: Ranger Suarez vs. Dylan Cease, odds and picks
items.snark: Suarez facing a team that doesn't swing and miss, Breslow's spreadsheets won't save this one.

items.headline: Patriots make first reported roster cut following 2026 draft
items.snark: Waived a WR and TE to make room for UDFAs. The churn never stops, baby.

items.headline: Patriots Hall of Fame finalists: Gronk, Mankins, Vinatieri
items.snark: Fans have until today to vote. If Gronk doesn't win this you can burn Foxborough to the ground.

items.headline: Behren Morton embraces backup QB role, opportunity with Patriots
items.snark: Seventh-round QB says he's a competitor who'll elevate the room. So does every seventh-round QB.

items.headline: Patriots UDFA tracker: Full list of undrafted rookie signings
items.snark: Vrabel wasn't around for Day 3 but the scouts kept working. Hope they picked the right Harvard lineman.

items.headline: Patriots UDFA tracker: Undrafted free agent signings after 2026 draft
items.snark: Patriots hoovering up UDFAs like Belichick never left, Vrabel skipped Saturday for counseling though.

items.headline: Mike Vrabel returns to Patriots after missing Day 3 of draft
items.snark: Vrabel's back at work after counseling. Priorities: family, football, not necessarily in that order on Saturdays.

items.headline: Patriots make first roster cut following 2026 draft
items.snark: Drafted nine guys, cut one immediately, the circle of life at Gillette.

items.headline: Patriots double down on OT depth: Lomu and Crownover takeaways
items.snark: Drafted four tackles in two years, Will Campbell must be thrilled about his job security.

items.headline: Patriots didn't consult Mike Vrabel on Day 3 of draft amid scandal
items.snark: Vrabel went to counseling, Eliot Wolf ran the show solo, somehow we still drafted well.

items.headline: AJ Brown trade speculation: Eagles draft moves point to Patriots deal
items.snark: Eagles drafted a WR, Patriots didn't, June 1 trade smoke getting thicc.

items.headline: Patriots vs. consensus big board: Draft steals and reaches
items.snark: Media nerds grading picks like they know what they're doing, Lomu at 28 was highway robbery though.
